etymology.description: semantic 隹(隹) (zhuī, 'bird') + phonetic 暵(暵) (hàn) -> originally depicted a type of bird -> phonetically borrowed for 'difficult'
etymology.interpretation: The character 难(難) (nán, 'difficult') combines the semantic component 隹(隹) (zhuī, 'bird') with the phonetic 暵(暵) (hàn). It originally depicted a specific type of bird, but was phonetically borrowed to express 'difficult'. This phonetic borrowing extends the meaning beyond its literal components.
etymology.simplification: The traditional character 難 was simplified by eliminating the left component and retaining 又(又) (yòu, 'again').

examples.chinese: 这道题很难(這道題很難)。
他觉得学中文很难(他覺得學中文很難)，但是他会努力(但是他會努力)。
examples.pinyin: Tā juéde xué Zhōngwén hěn nán, dànshì tā huì nǔlì.
examples.english: He thinks learning Chinese is difficult, but he will try hard.

examples.chinese: 解决这个问题很难(解決這個問題很難)，我们需要更多时间(我們需要更多時間)。
examples.pinyin: Jiějué zhège wèntí hěn nán, wǒmen xūyào gèng duō shíjiān.
examples.english: It's difficult to solve this problem, we need more time.
